title: Project metadata and command entry

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Project metadata and command entry; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Python Packaging User Guide; accessed 2026-07; Virtual Environments, Writing pyproject.toml, Packaging Projects, and Command-Line Tools guides; atomic topic: Project metadata and command entry
sources.claim: pyproject.toml records build-system requirements and project metadata, while a conventional source/test layout and declared command entry point make a package buildable and invocable reproducibly.

canonicalExplanation.summary: pyproject.toml records build-system requirements and project metadata, while a conventional source/test layout and declared command entry point make a package buildable and invocable reproducibly.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A src-layout project declares its build backend, project name/version/dependencies, and a console script that calls package.cli:main. Your goal is to package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Function contracts and returns; Interpreter and environment; Virtual environments and dependencies; Scripts, indentation and main guard. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: pyproject.toml records build-system requirements and project metadata, while a conventional source/test layout and declared command entry point make a package buildable and invocable reproducibly. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Metadata does not lock every application dependency, build backends differ, editable installs can hide packaging mistakes, package discovery needs configuration, and generated artifacts need clean-build checks. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Adding any pyproject.toml automatically discovers every module, locks all dependencies, and proves the built wheel contains the working tree.

includes: Use pyproject metadata and a conventional source/test layout for a small application.
Package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly.
excludes: Metadata does not lock every application dependency, build backends differ, editable installs can hide packaging mistakes, package discovery needs configuration, and generated artifacts need clean-build checks.

outcomes: Package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly.

examples.title: Project metadata and command entry: direct language case
examples.situation: A src-layout project declares its build backend, project name/version/dependencies, and a console script that calls package.cli:main.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A src-layout project declares its build backend, project name/version/dependencies, and a console script that calls package.cli:main.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Treat pyproject metadata and backend configuration as a build contract, then inspect and test the built artifact in a fresh environment.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Treat pyproject metadata and backend configuration as a build contract, then inspect and test the built artifact in a fresh environment.

examples.title: Project metadata and command entry: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A wheel is built in an isolated clean environment and installed into a fresh venv before the CLI is tested, exposing a missing package-data declaration.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A wheel is built in an isolated clean environment and installed into a fresh venv before the CLI is tested, exposing a missing package-data declaration.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Metadata does not lock every application dependency, build backends differ, editable installs can hide packaging mistakes, package discovery needs configuration, and generated artifacts need clean-build checks.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Adding any pyproject.toml automatically discovers every module, locks all dependencies, and proves the built wheel contains the working tree.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Adding any pyproject.toml automatically discovers every module, locks all dependencies, and proves the built wheel contains the working tree.
misconceptions.correction: Treat pyproject metadata and backend configuration as a build contract, then inspect and test the built artifact in a fresh environment.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Adding any pyproject.toml automatically discovers every module, locks all dependencies, and proves the built wheel contains the working tree.

analogy.example: a library whose catalogs, shelves, isolated rooms, and shipping manifest separate import names from installable distributions can introduce Project metadata and command entry, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: imports, packages, virtual environments, and pyproject builds
limitations: sys.path, partial module initialization, build backends, editable installs, and wheel contents need direct inspection

recap.summary: You should now be able to package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly. Keep this boundary in view: Metadata does not lock every application dependency, build backends differ, editable installs can hide packaging mistakes, package discovery needs configuration, and generated artifacts need clean-build checks.
